test.detail: 324 tests passed (281 vera_core + 33 vera_eval + 9 vera_cli + 1 vera_mcp)

nonBlockingIssues.description: VectorStore INSERT OR REPLACE on chunk_id_map with AUTOINCREMENT causes rowid changes that orphan old vectors. Current pipeline safe (clear() first), but will break incremental re-insertion. Fix before incremental-indexing feature.
sanitize_error_message uses &msg[..500] which panics on multi-byte UTF-8 boundary. Low probability but a correctness bug in error handling path.
EmbeddingProviderConfig derives Debug which would expose api_key if debug-formatted. Should implement custom Debug with redaction.
nDCG computation allows values >1.0 due to duplicate ground-truth matching. Doesn't affect required assertions but makes nDCG unreliable for cross-tool comparison.
vector-only mode in benchmark script is a no-op. Correctly worked around via M1 baselines, but misleading if someone tries to use --modes vector-only.
59.2s benchmark for 175K LOC is within 1.3% of 60s target with ±15s API variability. Target is not reliably met on every run due to external API dependency.

Python class methods are chunked as part of parent class, not as individual chunks. Reduces symbol lookup accuracy for Python methods. Documented as known limitation; the maintainability-and-cleanup feature has a task to fix this.
Class-method extraction asymmetry: only Rust impl methods are individually chunked; Java/TS/Python/C++ class methods remain part of the parent class chunk. Design decision not documented.
BM25 and vector search run sequentially. Parallelizing them (tokio::spawn_blocking for BM25) would reduce query latency. Future performance optimization.

Several files exceed 500-line hard limit due to inline test modules (extractor.rs: 597, metadata_tests.rs: 1064, provider.rs: 557, vector.rs: 638, reranker.rs: 875, search_quality_tests.rs: 1028, types.rs: 570, main.rs: 551). Most are justified by comprehensive test suites; some commit messages lack the required explanation.

appliedUpdates.description: Added .vera/ index directory convention, Matryoshka truncation details, chunk line encoding, tuned embedding defaults, method extraction asymmetry, post-retrieval filtering pattern, and M2 benchmark results to architecture.md
Added Nebius API quirks section to environment.md: transient HTTP 400 'Unable to process' errors, rate limit behavior at 8+ concurrent requests, auth error non-retry policy

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify file size budget for test code: Add note that the 500-line hard limit applies primarily to production code. Files where test modules push total lines over 500 are acceptable when production code is within the 300-line soft target and the commit message documents the overage. Alternatively, exempt #[cfg(test)] modules from the line count.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 7+ reviewers flagged file size overages caused by inline test modules: extractor.rs (597, 325 prod + 272 test), provider.rs (557, 480 prod + 77 test), vector.rs (638, 209 prod + 429 test), reranker.rs (875, 515 prod + 360 test). All have production code within budget. Some commit messages lack required explanation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax strict TDD mandate in implementation-worker skill. Change 'Write Failing Tests First' to allow test-adjacent development where tests and implementation are written together. This is the de facto practice across all 13 features — no worker wrote failing tests first. Rust's colocated test modules make this impractical. Suggested wording: 'Write tests alongside or before implementation. For modules with internal types (API surfaces, data structures), developing tests and code together is acceptable. Strict red-green TDD applies when extending existing APIs with well-defined interfaces.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 13 feature reviewers noted TDD deviation. Workers wrote implementation and tests together in every case. Handoff skillFeedback from 9+ features explicitly suggests relaxing this. Rust's #[cfg(test)] mod pattern encourages colocated tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document async trait convention: Vera uses #[allow(async_fn_in_trait)] for EmbeddingProvider and Reranker traits. This makes them not dyn-compatible (static dispatch only). Future workers should follow this pattern for new async traits unless dynamic dispatch is needed (in which case, use the async-trait crate).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: embedding/provider.rs and retrieval/reranker.rs both use #[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]. Two reviewers flagged the undocumented convention. This affects how future traits can be used.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add security convention: Types containing secrets (API keys, tokens) must NOT derive Debug. Implement a custom Debug that redacts secret fields. Current EmbeddingProviderConfig derives Debug with api_key field — if debug-formatted, the key would leak.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: embedding-pipeline reviewer flagged EmbeddingProviderConfig deriving Debug with api_key. AGENTS.md says 'NEVER log, print, or expose API keys' but doesn't provide guidance on how to handle Debug derives on secret-containing types.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document CLI error handling convention: CLI commands use eprintln + process::exit(1) for user-facing errors, bypassing Rust's normal Result return path. This is the established pattern across index, search, update commands. Future CLI workers should follow this pattern for consistency.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: index-command and hybrid-retrieval reviewers both noted the process::exit(1) pattern. main.rs has 9+ process::exit calls. The pattern is consistent but undocumented.

rejectedObservations.observation: CI ambiguity — 'Add CI basics' interpreted as local tooling vs pipeline config
rejectedObservations.reason: Not applicable — project does not use CI pipelines, and local cargo test/clippy/fmt is the correct interpretation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Forward-looking services.yaml MCP command that doesn't work yet
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented and expected — MCP server is a later milestone feature.

rejectedObservations.observation: OptionalExt trait duplicated in metadata.rs and vector.rs
rejectedObservations.reason: Code cleanup issue, not knowledge or guidance. Will be addressed naturally during maintainability-and-cleanup feature.
Already exempt per AGENTS.md: 'spikes/ and benchmarks/scripts/ directories are exempt from size budgets'.

rejectedObservations.observation: BM25 ranking relies on Tantivy's natural length normalization for symbol_name field boost
rejectedObservations.reason: Implicit but correct behavior — BM25 inherently favors shorter fields. Documenting this would be over-engineering; the test suite empirically verifies the ranking.

rejectedObservations.observation: Missing PartialEq derives on Chunk/SearchResult types
rejectedObservations.reason: Standard code suggestion, not a knowledge or guidance issue. Workers can add derives as needed.
